review_text: I am extremely dissatisfied with this product. First of all, there was absolutely no effect of the ones listed in the description. Secondly, the tablets are difficult to swallow (big) and although I do try to be understanding about this (assuming that there's a reason they can't make them smaller), they also have a detestable taste... I've taken other magnesium, and other supplements, and none of them tasted that badly. Most of them taste bad, but this one is the worst, really. Thirdly, since I have taken other magnesium before (different forms of magnesium; such as citrate, l-threonate and such and such), I *always* felt a nice sensation throughout my entire body while I was taking them; especially in the beginning. I can't describe this better, but I believe it's because of my lack of magnesium (and how good it feels when I finally take it; same thing with eating fruits and whatnot). However with this magnesium I've felt... absolutely nothing. I didn't feel anything at the beginning, or while taking it (after a few days). I also didn't feel the good effects it was supposed to have... All in all, I am very dissatisfied, and I regret buying it. I do not know if it is the specific magnesium to blame, or the label (Doctor's Best)... For the time being I won't order again from this label.

review_title: Best B-Complex I've tried - no niacin flush!
review_text: After using this B-Complex for 6 weeks, I can confidently say the quality is exceptional. Life Extension really delivers with their ingredient selection - they use the bioactive forms of B vitamins, including methylfolate instead of regular folic acid, which makes a huge difference in absorption. The size of the capsules is moderate - not tiny but definitely manageable. What really impressed me is that despite having high-potency B vitamins, I experienced zero niacin flush, which has been an issue with other B complexes I've tried. The package is well-designed with a moisture-control packet to maintain potency. The effects on my energy levels have been remarkable but not overstimulating. It's more like a steady improvement in my overall vitality. The impact on my nervous system is noticeable - I feel more balanced, and my stress response seems better managed. My metabolism appears to be working more efficiently too - I have consistent energy throughout the day without crashes. A 2021 study in the Journal of Clinical Medicine demonstrated that supplementation with bioactive B vitamins improved cognitive function and energy metabolism significantly more than standard B vitamin forms. The taste is neutral, and there's no unpleasant vitamin aftertaste or burping, which can be common with B vitamins. The potency is clearly high-quality - you can feel it's working, but it's not overwhelming. I take one capsule in the morning with breakfast, and the positive effects last all day. I especially love how it supports my energy without causing jitters or anxiety. My mental clarity has improved, and I'm handling busy days much better. This is definitely a keeper in my supplement routine!

review_title: Good magnesium form, but coating ingredient not safe.
review_text: My experience is that this Magnesium mineral form - magnesium lysinate glycinate chelate - is very well absorbed, better then other forms of magnesium in supplements.. However, I have a sensitive liver and I noticed a negative reaction to some of the other ingredients.. It is probably the coating, either the ""croscarmellose sodium" or the "hydroxypropyl cellulose" or both together that give me a bad reaction. Feel really weak and my legs seem to want to buckle, liver toxicity is happening in my opinion... The negative effects are mostly over after 24 hours. Positive effects are also experienced. So a bit of a difficult choice to make: do I take this or not? The package is a really good size and I do feel a deeper muscle relaxation, better sleep, so that is a benefit. Doctor`s best has mostly high quality products. Did not take it long enough to establish what it does for my joints. Not a bad taste in my experience. But would be really nice for me if Doctor`s best could put this " magnesium lysinate glycinate chelate" for in a capsule in stead of a tablet with questionable ingredients in the coating. The five stars for sure!!

review_title: Magnesium is critical to keeping you healthy.
review_text: Magnesium glycinate supplements offer a variety of potential benefits, including improved sleep, reduced anxiety, and enhanced muscle relaxation. It is also linked to better bone and heart health, and may help manage blood sugar levels. This form of magnesium is known for its high absorption rate and gentle effect on the digestive system. Taking magnesium glycinate daily is generally safe for most people, especially when adhering to recommended dosages. It's a well-tolerated form of magnesium, less likely to cause digestive issues than other forms. However, excessive intake can lead to side effects like diarrhea, nausea, and stomach cramps.

review_text: I have been using California Gold Nutrition Omega-3 Fish Oil for about three weeks and have had only average results. Each softgel contains 1000 mg of fish oil (180 mg EPA and 120 mg DHA), which is not a low dose. I also want to support heart and brain health, but in fact there is no obvious effect. There is no improvement in energy or concentration. What annoys me the most is that after swallowing it, there is a distinct fishy smell that comes back to my burps, which is really uncomfortable. The claim that it is "fishy-free" is not true at all. Although the soft capsules are made of fish gelatin, the quality seems good and 240 capsules are quite cost-effective, but the taste problem greatly affects the experience. It may work for some people, but I personally don't think it's worth repurchasing and can only give it two stars.

review_text: I don't know exactly why I chose this product. I think it was rated highly. But after about 15 minutes of taking this vitamin, I was overcome by a feeling of pain bordering on nausea. After sitting and taking several deep breaths to my back the pain began to lift but at the same time a wave of heat began to irradiate up my chest and to my ears and cheeks. I tried to cool my face with my hands, but when I looked in the mirror out of curiosity saw that my ears were bright red, and examining my chest found that it too was splotched all over. I wondered at first whether the shower I had taken was too hot, for my skin was everywhere red where the water may have touched it; my forearms were alive, itching with hives. I have never had any adverse reaction to any of the numerous vitamins I take, but looking at the bottle found that the label warns of just such a 'temporary reaction.' and incredibly high dosages (833%, 12,500%) of things I wonder why anyone should need. As I never want another temporary reaction' like this again, these pills are going into the garbage.

review_text: Magnesium is an essential mineral that plays a pivotal role in more than 300 enzymatic reactions within the body. It directly affects muscle and nerve function, blood pressure regulation, and energy production. It also plays an important role in bone and heart health, contributing to the stability of normal heart rhythm and preventing muscle cramps. Studies show that magnesium deficiency is associated with an increased risk of heart disease, type 2 diabetes, high blood pressure, and migraines. Research also suggests that magnesium supplements may help improve sleep quality, reduce symptoms of depression and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of premenstrual syndrome in women.

review_title: Good value for money, although it contains many inactive molecular forms
review_text: It doesn't contain the most expensive, best quality ingredients, but importantly, it doesn't contain harmful molecular forms like so many cheaper products. It does, however, contain many inactive molecular forms such as Thiamine HCl (B1), Riboflavin (B2), Niacinamide, Pyridoxine HCl (B6), D-calcium anthothenate. However, it also contains biologically active molecular forms (with excellent bioavailability) such as Riboflavin 5'-phosphate (B2), Pyridoxal 5'-phosphate (B6), L-5-methyltetrahydrofolate calcium salt (B, Biotin (in its inactive form), methylcobamalin (B12). review_text: I've bought this product at least twice and was planning to continue. However, today (2025.0904), I ran an AI analysis and concluded the following: The content of multiple B vitamins in this product far exceeds the daily value (DV) of our diet, reaching therapeutic-grade, ultra-high doses. It's not suitable for daily health care, but rather for specific health needs (such as severe B vitamin deficiency, energy metabolism support, etc.) and should only be used under the guidance of a doctor or nutritionist. Does it exceed the DV? Unequivocally, yes. The doses of many ingredients are dozens or even hundreds of times the DV. Q: Can I take it? A: Exercise caution! If you haven't been examined by a doctor and have been specifically advised to take such high doses of B vitamins, do not purchase or take this product. For the vast majority of ordinary people looking to supplement their diet, they should choose a multivitamin or B vitamin product with a dose close to 100% DV. If you are taking any other medications or have a health condition, it's crucial to consult a doctor before use, as high doses of B vitamins may interact with certain medications. In short: This product is a "strong medicine," not a "health supplement." Avoid blind use. Therefore, it is not recommended for daily use. Everyone should be aware of the consequences of excessive supplementation: Vitamin B6 may cause severe peripheral neuritis, resulting in symptoms such as unsteady gait and numbness in the hands and feet; it may also cause allergic reactions such as rashes and itching. Vitamin B1 overdose is relatively rare and generally has low toxicity. However, long-term, excessive intake (such as far exceeding the recommended amount shown in the table) may cause headaches, fatigue, irritability, dizziness, swelling, and cardiac arrhythmias.

The fish gelatin softgels are easy to swallow and have no fishy aftertaste, making them a great choice for those looking for a potent and high-quality Omega-3 supplement. As it contains only 180 mg EPA and 120 mg DHA per softgel, it is a basic fish oil supplement rather than a high-potency one. While it still provides essential Omega-3s, the dosage per softgel is relatively low compared to concentrated formulas. For those seeking stronger cardiovascular, brain, and joint support, a supplement with higher EPA/DHA content per softgel might be a better option.

review_title: product review Life Extension, BioActive Complete B-Complex, 60 Vegetarian Capsules
review_text: Life Extension, BioActive Complete B-Complex, 60 Vegetarian Capsules The Life Extension BioActive Complete B-Complex is a high-quality B-vitamin complex that effectively helps replenish vitamin deficiencies and alleviate associated symptoms. With regular use of this product, I noticed a significant improvement in my overall well-being, and it helped reduce the feelings of sadness and depressive symptoms caused by B-vitamin deficiency. Additionally, my nerve-related issues, particularly the numbness and pain in my hands and fingers, have significantly diminished. One of the greatest advantages of this product is that the B12 vitamin it contains is in the form of methylcobalamin, rather than the commonly used cyanocobalamin, which contains cyanide. This more natural form is not only more effective but also safer, which is particularly important to me as I prefer supplements with the most natural ingredients possible. The box contains 60 vegetarian capsules that are easy to take, and the daily dosage fits seamlessly into my daily routine. The price-to-value ratio is also excellent, considering the high quality and effectiveness of the product. Overall, I am very satisfied with the Life Extension BioActive Complete B-Complex and highly recommend it to anyone experiencing similar symptoms or simply looking to support their body's vitamin intake. This product truly contributes to a more balanced and healthier life.

review_title: Handle with care - Affected me badly
review_text: I purchased this B Complex product, took it for 3 months. I ended up with numb hands at night, and sometimes while sitting and watching movies or tv. I read about many people in Australia having health problems and poisoning themselves after taking high levels of Vitamin B6. Google search: "Vitamin B6 toxicity is caused by taking excessive amounts of B6-containing supplements. This can cause peripheral neuropathy. The symptoms of peripheral neuropathy include numbness, tingling, pain, and weakness in the hands and feet." When I checked the % daily value of B6 in this product, it is 5,882% of RDA! It also has high levels of B12 12,500%; B5 10,000%;1 8,333%; B2 5,769%; B7 3,333%. I have stopped taking these capsules, and I don't have numb fingers anymore. Handle with Care! Be extremely careful!

review_text: I ordered this dietary supplement due to the fact that everywhere they talk about the benefits of omega-3. I ordered it, tried it, nothing changed. I became interested in the topic and it turned out that recent studies show that: 1. none of the dietary supplements on the market has shown effectiveness 2. Omega is unstable, which is why it quickly breaks down "outside of food", in other words, if you want to get omega 3 - eat more fishes. 3. in addition, omega from dietary supplements does not break down very well, as a result of which, instead of benefit, you get harm (in the form of free radicals, which, according to many gurus, PP omega 3 helps to remove.but alas, in reality, a completely different picture is obtained 4. Despite stories of how important it is for the body to take omega-3 drugs, research shows that this is not at all the case. Bottom line, modern evidence-based medicine tells us that such dietary supplements are not needed. But the choice is always yours

During that time, I didn't feel any clearer bowel movements, and on the contrary, I didn't have any constipation, diarrhea, or stomach pains. (I didn't eat much junk food to begin with, and I didn't have a lot of lactic acid bacteria in my diet.) Maybe the bacteria have finally settled in my intestines and my condition has improved, but when I think about the days when my farts got worse, I was so scared to go out and meet people, and I didn't feel any particular benefits, so I'm going to go back to domestic lactic acid bacteria supplements. review_title: My Experience with Life Extension's BioActive Complete B-Complex
review_text: Having tried various B-complex supplements, I've found Life Extension's BioActive Complete B-Complex to be a standout product. What impresses me most is its exceptional price-to-performance ratio - you're getting comprehensive B vitamin support without breaking the bank. Each bottle contains 60 vegetarian capsules, which is a good monthly supply at the recommended dosage. The formula includes the complete spectrum of B vitamins in their bioactive forms, which means better absorption and utilization by the body compared to standard B vitamins. I've noticed steady energy levels throughout the day since starting this supplement, without the crash that comes from some energy supplements. The bioactive forms are particularly beneficial for those of us who may have genetic variations affecting B vitamin metabolism. What makes this product such a good value is that you're getting premium-quality, bioavailable forms of these essential nutrients at a price point typically associated with lower-quality B-complexes. Many comparable products with methylated B vitamins cost significantly more. If you're looking for a comprehensive B-complex that delivers results without excessive cost, Life Extension's formula deserves consideration. It's been a reliable addition to my supplement regimen.

As a result, it was 50 mg per tablet. I was having localized numbness all over my body on a daily basis, and even though I had a blood test, I couldn't identify the cause. Despite the lack of information gathering, I realized that overdose would have some kind of effect, so I decided to purchase it after researching the appropriate amount (or upper limit).

review_text: I thought this was a good company, was getting many supplements from them. I will not buy anything ever again. They have a dose of 100mg B6. Read the following and you'll be the judge: B6 neuropathy, or peripheral neuropathy, is nerve damage in the hands and feet (tingling, numbness, pain) caused by excessive intake of vitamin B6 (pyridoxine) supplements, even at doses below 100mg/day, though high doses are more common culprits, and it's often found in multivitamins. Symptoms can worsen with continued use, potentially leading to balance issues and difficulty walking, so checking supplement labels and consulting a doctor is crucial before taking high-dose B6.

review_text: I purchased two probiotics: Garden of Life, Dr. Formulated Probiotics and California COLD Nutritiob LactoBif. Conducted my own investigation: I bought whole milk with a shelf life of 7 days. Warmed up a little until warm, poured into three glasses, added California COLD Nutritiob LactoBif to one, Garden of Life, Dr. Formulated Probiotics, the third milk glass is a control sample. A day later, I saw this result: in the control glass, tender kefir, in a glass with Garden of Life, Dr. Formulated Probiotics is a very thick buttermilk (you can melt a spoon), and in a glass with California COLD Nutritiob LactoBif everything is also milk, as it was a day ago !!!! This is not a probiotic, but some kind of preservative that stopped any acidification of milk! Be careful! Check probiotics at least in this way!

review_text: This is my first review. This is the first time I've had a noticeable problem after taking a supplement, so I couldn't help but write it. There were some that were neither good nor bad, but this time I'm suffering. I used to have a bowel movement 2-3 times a day, but before I knew it, I was constipated. However, I still have to go to the toilet, so I have to rush there countless times. Even when I do go, it's a sludgy, shapeless mess. I didn't think it was because of LactoBif, so I took one box and then started on the second. I never thought that lactic acid bacteria would cause constipation, and I regret that I realized it too late. It can't be helped if it doesn't suit my constitution, but I hope that no one has to suffer.

review_text: In an innovative article published in Forbes it turns out that niacin (vitamin B3) which exists as a vitamin both by itself and in the B complex can increase the risk of heart disease in high doses as published in February 2024 in Forbes. The researchers did not rule it out, but saw the need for more regulation regarding it. It turns out that high levels of it can cause inflammation and damage of blood vessels and also increase the risk of heart disease. The study was published in Nature Medicine. The preparation is very old and was used in high cholesterol. Its recommended daily dose for men is 16 mg and for women - 14 mg. 18 mg in pregnant women and 17 mg in lactating women. It is present in many foods and in the past it was used for eating and less as a food supplement. High doses of it can, as mentioned, cause damage. I stopped taking it in food supplements alone or in a complex because of its high dose in food supplements and to get it in food I looked in Ayherb for a low dose and I didn't find it. The solution is to switch to a low production of it in all types of preparations. That's why I stopped the remaining niacin preparations that I purchased in the past and I hope that over time the issue will be arranged

review_title: My neuropathy increased due to excess vitamin B6
review_text: I have peripheral neuropathy so I must take vitamin B supplements for proper nerve function. The fact is that I took a little more than one bottle and felt a lot of pain and numbness in my feet and hands. I also experienced difficulty falling asleep. I started researching and found that excess vitamin B6 causes neuropathy or aggravates it when it is in the form of pyridoxine hydrochloride. In its P5P form it is harmless, what happens is that this supplement has high amounts of that vitamin in its 2 forms but it does not specify what amounts it has of each one. To conclude, this supplement did not work for me, but I still buy other supplements this brand that seems good to me despite everything.

review_text: Omega-3 fatty acid is extremely useful for our health. But we must also know that when oxidized, it turns into a trans fat. Therefore, good cleaning, transportation and storage conditions are critical for omega-3. And it is also desirable to take omega simultaneously with antioxidants, which will prevent oxidation. Check whether your omega is very good: put the capsule in the freezer. Good fat will freeze and remain completely transparent. Oxidized (ie already transformed into trans fat) - it will become cloudy when frozen. This fish oil at me has grown turbid (though stored in a refrigeration cabinet). So he flew into the trash. It would be desirable, of course, to save - the price is very attractive. But health is more expensive. Be careful, check your omega freeze. And do not skimp on yourself and your health. UPDATE. I bought an omega Nordic - it passed the freeze test.
